human_verification.notes: Reviewed child-care-specific boundary controls, reporting-system dependencies, provider-status and privacy caveats, metric-definition constraints, functionality mapping, and retained-implementation promotion limits for issue #1181.

review_notes: All required scenario families for child-care analytics and reporting are covered.
All declared abilities are mapped to passing coverage and represented in results.
No retained implementation claim is made without separate promotion context.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls prohibit subsidy-award, licensing, safety, legal, and disclosure-authority overreach while enabling child-care analytics evidence handling.
Sources and scenario coverage include public OCC, ChildCare.gov, Head Start, licensing, market-rate, and confidentiality references with explicit refresh and conflict handling.

Scenario set covers denominator disputes, stale data escalation, provider-status conflicts, privacy blockers, unsupported benchmark refusal, child-care-specific in-scope execution, and explicit routing to adjacent specialists.
notes: Runtime remains `specification-based`; retained-implementation promotion requires a separate governance-approved build path.
Tenant-supplied jurisdiction, provider-type map, metric dictionary, source-precedence map, disclosure approvals, and adjacent-owner map are required for high-confidence recommendations.
